name: Sentry
description: Application error and performance monitoring platform. Yields evidence of error-handling and alerting discipline: organisation audit logs, alert rule configuration, SSO/2FA enforcement settings and error trend exports.

steps.action: Navigate to Sentry > Organization Settings > Audit Log to view administrative changes across the organisation
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated evidence-collection account with the minimum role that can view the audit log, not a shared Owner login
steps.audit_note: Audit log entries are timestamped by Sentry and cannot be edited by users. There is no built-in CSV export of the audit log, so capture dated screenshots or print-to-PDF for the collection period

steps.action: Use the Select Action filter on the Audit Log to evidence specific control activity, e.g. org.edit for changes to the require_2fa setting and member.pending for removed members
steps.audit_note: Filtered views scoped to the collection period demonstrate continuous monitoring of privileged changes; ensure date ranges leave no gaps between collections

steps.action: Capture screenshots of Organization Settings > General Settings > Security & Privacy (Require Two-Factor Authentication toggle) and Organization Settings > Auth (configured SSO provider)
steps.audit_note: Include the organisation slug and current date in each screenshot to establish provenance

steps.action: Navigate to Alerts > Alert Rules and capture the list of configured issue and metric alert rules, including owners and notification targets
steps.permissions_required: Member (view); Admin or above to see all projects
steps.audit_note: Alert rule configuration is evidence that error conditions are monitored and routed to responders

steps.action: Open Explore > Discover, run your saved error-trend query for the collection period and click Export to download the results as CSV
steps.audit_note: Large exports are delivered by email as a download link; save the CSV with a date-stamped filename covering the query period

steps.action: Upload the screenshots and CSV exports to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Review exports for personal data, DSN values or tokens captured in error events before uploading

steps.action: In User Settings > Notifications, enable Weekly Reports for the evidence-collection account; Sentry emails an organisation activity summary every Saturday
steps.security_note: Deliver reports to a restricted, monitored compliance mailbox rather than personal inboxes

steps.action: Create and save Discover queries for the error-trend views you report on, so weekly exports use identical fields and filters every time
steps.audit_note: Saved queries are shared organisation-wide, giving a consistent, repeatable definition of the evidence dataset

steps.action: Configure issue and metric alert rules to notify a retained channel (compliance mailbox or dedicated Slack channel) so alert deliveries form a continuous evidence trail
steps.audit_note: Retained alert notifications evidence that alerting operated throughout the period, not just at collection time

steps.action: File the weekly report emails into the evidence repository on receipt. Note that Sentry has no user-schedulable CSV export, so the weekly report email is the only built-in scheduled report — set a recurring calendar check to catch missed weeks
steps.audit_note: Record any missed weekly reports and the reason; unexplained gaps weaken the evidence trail

steps.action: Create an internal integration in Organization Settings > Developer Settings > Custom Integrations > New Internal Integration, granting only the read scopes org:read, event:read and member:read
steps.security_note: Treat the integration token as a production secret: store it in a secrets manager, never in code or config files, and rotate it on a defined schedule
steps.audit_note: Integration creation and token events are recorded in the organisation Audit Log

steps.action: Call GET /api/0/organizations/{organization_id_or_slug}/events/ with dataset=errors, the field list you report on, and statsPeriod (e.g. 24h) to export error trend data for the period
steps.audit_note: Store the raw JSON response with the request parameters so the export is reproducible

steps.action: Call GET /api/0/organizations/{organization_id_or_slug}/stats_v2/ to collect event volume counts per project as a completeness baseline
Call GET /api/0/organizations/{organization_id_or_slug}/members/ to export the member list with roles and pending invitations for access review evidence
steps.audit_note: Periodic member exports support user access review controls alongside the SSO/2FA settings evidence

steps.action: Schedule the collection script daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, reading the token from the secrets manager at runtime, with retries and alerting on failed runs
steps.security_note: Restrict pipeline logs so the Authorization header is never printed; failed-run alerts should go to the team that owns evidence collection

steps.action: Validate each run: compare collected event counts against the stats_v2 baseline and flag drops below the expected volume
steps.audit_note: Sentry's organisation audit log endpoint is not part of the published API, so continue collecting audit log evidence via the console (L1 step 1) until Sentry publishes it

L4.title: Sentry Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Enable webhooks on your internal integration (Organization Settings > Developer Settings > select the integration, set the webhook URL) and subscribe to issue and error events, including alert rule actions
steps.security_note: Serve the webhook endpoint over TLS 1.2+ and verify the Sentry-Hook-Signature header on every delivery before trusting the payload

steps.action: Enable Data Forwarding per project (Project Settings > Data Forwarding) to stream raw error events to Segment, Splunk or Amazon SQS for SIEM retention
steps.permissions_required: Admin or above; Data Forwarding is available on Business and Enterprise plans
steps.audit_note: SIEM-side retention with immutable storage strengthens the evidentiary value of forwarded events

steps.action: Reconcile received webhook and forwarded event counts daily against GET /api/0/organizations/{organization_id_or_slug}/stats_v2/ to measure collection completeness
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics (received vs reported counts) are the core evidence that the pipeline captures everything Sentry emits

steps.action: Implement automated quality checks on the pipeline: validate payload schema versions, detect gaps in the event time series and flag duplicate deliveries
Build a collection-health dashboard (events/day per project, delivery latency, coverage %, failed webhook deliveries) and alert when any metric breaches its threshold
steps.audit_note: The dashboard itself is continuous evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection-health report summarising completeness, gaps and remediation, and file it in the evidence repository
